name: Red Holloway
abstract: James Wesley "Red" Holloway (May 31, 1927 – February 25, 2012) was an American jazz saxophonist.

interviews.title: Red Holloway interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 4, 1995

concerts.label: Harold Arlen Centennial Celebration
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Hendricks
about_quotes.evidence: I opened at the Blue Note with Clark Terry, Benny Golson, Al Grey, and Red Holloway to do that album that’s now out, called Boppin’ at the Blue Note.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Hendricks — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: There’s Red Holloway, Teddy Edwards. There’s some great guys around here that they’re stylists, that have a good sound.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harry Edison — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Howard Alden
about_quotes.evidence: Red Holloway and Sweets Edison would be playing — they’re friends of mine, I play with them, we get along fine
about_quotes.doc_title: Howard Alden interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: George Benson
about_quotes.evidence: Red Holloway, he always got on me... We had an exciting two-and-a-half years. It was contentious.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Benson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
